metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Grade 9 Applied Design, Skills and Technologies builds on Grade 8 foundations with greater depth and independence across the same range of disciplines. Students tackle more complex design challenges, apply iterative thinking to refine solutions, and develop increasing precision in their technical and creative work. They explore career connections and begin to specialize in areas of interest.
metadata.general_description: Core curriculum with deeper application, user-centred design thinking, and increasingly independent project design.

big_ideas.statement: Iterative design and user-centred thinking lead to more effective and meaningful solutions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn that great solutions come from multiple cycles of design, testing, and refinement. By keeping the user's perspective at the center of their work, they create designs that truly solve problems and meet real needs.

big_ideas.statement: Precision, quality, and technical mastery are developed through practice, feedback, and reflection.
big_ideas.elaboration: As students apply skills with greater depth in their chosen disciplines, they discover that excellence comes from attention to detail, willingness to practice, and ability to learn from both successes and failures.

big_ideas.statement: Technology and design are interconnected tools for creating, communicating, and solving problems.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students recognize that digital tools, physical technologies, and creative design thinking work together to amplify human capability and enable innovation across all disciplines.

big_ideas.statement: Design and technology choices have social, environmental, and economic consequences that matter.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop critical awareness of how their design decisions affect users, communities, and the planet, leading to more responsible and thoughtful creation.

competencies.raw: Apply iterative design processes to complex challenges
i_can_statements: I can break down complex design problems into manageable parts
I can conduct user research to understand needs and constraints deeply
I can create multiple design iterations based on feedback and testing
I can use data and observation to justify design decisions
I can explain the reasoning behind my design choices
I can balance aesthetic, functional, and practical considerations
I can adapt my design based on changing requirements or constraints
I can evaluate my solution against success criteria and user feedback

competencies.raw: Consider broader impact in design thinking
i_can_statements: I can identify social and environmental impacts of design choices
I can research materials and processes that are sustainable and ethical
I can design for inclusivity and accessibility
I can engage with Indigenous perspectives and approaches to design

competencies.raw: Present and advocate for design solutions
i_can_statements: I can create compelling presentations of my design work
I can defend my design choices with evidence and reasoning
I can communicate with different audiences about my work
I can accept and integrate constructive criticism

competencies.raw: Apply advanced techniques with increasing precision
i_can_statements: I can execute complex techniques with accuracy and consistency
I can troubleshoot quality issues and refine my approach
I can manage precision requirements for different projects
I can adapt techniques for different materials and contexts
I can plan and manage a multi-step project from start to finish
I can work through challenges without constant supervision
I can manage my time effectively to meet deadlines
I can self-assess my work and identify areas for improvement
I can explain the nuances of my chosen discipline
I can apply advanced skills specific to my area of focus
I can explore career pathways related to my interests
I can teach others basic skills in my area of expertise
I can take on different roles in group projects (leader, contributor, etc.)
I can manage conflicts and differences constructively
I can coordinate complex tasks across a team
I can document and communicate group progress

competencies.raw: Design and build complex technological systems
i_can_statements: I can integrate multiple technologies to solve a problem
I can design systems with consideration for efficiency and sustainability
I can troubleshoot and optimize technological solutions
I can document and explain how systems work
I can write code to control physical systems or solve digital problems
I can debug code and troubleshoot logic errors
I can use digital tools and software with fluency
I can understand how algorithms and data structures work
I can research and compare different technological options
I can evaluate technologies based on criteria like cost, effectiveness, and sustainability
I can understand limitations and potential risks of technologies
I can make informed recommendations about technology use

competencies.raw: Engage critically with technology and society
i_can_statements: I can analyze how technology shapes and is shaped by culture and society
I can identify ethical issues related to technology development and use
I can use technology responsibly and advocate for digital citizenship
I can explore how different communities use and value technology differently

areas.name: Advanced Design Processes
topics: Design for sustainability and social responsibility

areas.name: Technical Skill Development
topics: Material science and properties at deeper levels

areas.name: Digital Tools and Technologies
topics: Advanced digital design and CAD software
